essence: Uses AI, predictive analytics, and sensor data to maintain, diagnose, and optimise manufacturing equipment before failures occur.

identity.title: AI Maintenance Engineer
identity.summary: Implements AI‑driven predictive maintenance, monitors machine health in real time, and ensures equipment reliability in advanced manufacturing environments.

salary.notes: Engineers in aerospace, robotics, or fully autonomous smart‑factory environments can exceed £135k-£180k+.

qualifications: Experience in maintenance engineering, automation, or digital manufacturing
Knowledge of predictive maintenance, sensors, and machine‑health analytics
Skills in ML models for anomaly detection and failure prediction
Understanding of CNC, robotics, and Industry 4.0 systems

lifestyle.description: AI maintenance engineers work in aerospace, robotics, automotive, and Industry 4.0 environments. They use AI to predict failures, schedule maintenance proactively, and ensure equipment reliability. The role suits individuals who enjoy diagnostics, optimisation, and high‑tech engineering.

habits: Monitors machine health using sensors and analytics
Diagnoses issues before they cause downtime
Collaborates with production, AI, and automation teams
Ensures reliability and uptime in autonomous workflows
